body.title: FAIL 2026-07-13: 149 tickets cleared the design step with no intention — kicked back. The learning is that this was ONE hole, not 149 oversights.
body.cp: CP2 — FAIL = Further Advance In Learning
body.what_happened: 149 sprint tickets (approved, queued, dispatchable) carried NO intention. Under D-intentions-are-the-root + proof-on-close, the intention IS the property mutation-red breaks — so with no intention there is no property to break and the ticket CANNOT BE PROVEN. They could only ever have closed hollow. All 149 were returned to `triage` (the design step) as FAIL; 1 more (T-gate-timeout-test) was cancelled as test junk that leaked into the live queue.
body.the_disposition_and_why_it_matters: CC's first instinct was to DRAFT the missing intentions ticket by ticket. Akien overruled: 'those should get sent back to the design step as FAIL for learning from and fixing.' THIS IS THE ANTI-ESCAPE-HATCH MOVE. Backfilling 149 intentions would have PAPERED OVER the actual defect and produced a green queue that means nothing — the exact failure this whole line of work exists to kill. Per CLAUDE.md: the nuisance stays VISIBLE so it drives finding the fix; papered over, it sucks silently forever (CP4). A ticket with no intention is NOT defective work. IT IS INCOMPLETE DESIGN, and incomplete design goes back to design.

THE LEARNING — ONE HOLE, NOT 149 OVERSIG.root_cause: The triage→sprint transition NEVER CHECKED for an intention. The field was optional at `add`, and nothing gated promotion. 149 tickets did not each get forgotten; ONE GATE WAS ABSENT, and it let everything through. Counting the instances instead of finding the gate is how you 'fix' this 149 times and still ship the 150th.
THE LEARNING — ONE HOLE, NOT 149 OVERSIG.the_compounding_cause: The intent extractor was SUPPOSED to soften this by inferring an intention at `add` time — and it has been ~99% BROKEN since it shipped (2,436/2,470 predictions are an except-block fallback). But note carefully: EVEN WORKING, IT WOULD NOT HAVE FIXED THIS. A machine-extracted intention is a RATCHET, not a SPEC (D-provenance-is-the-safety-property-2026-07-13); only a DECLARED intention gates proof-on-close. So the extractor was never the gate, and treating it as one was a second, quieter error.
THE LEARNING — ONE HOLE, NOT 149 OVERSIG.the_pattern_underneath: Reading the 8 highest-priority of the 149, nearly EVERY ONE is the same defect wearing different clothes: A SIGNAL THAT CANNOT DISTINGUISH TWO CAUSES. Capability vs budget (T-capability-measured-at-a-budget-ceiling). Truncation vs inability (T-escalation-truncation-misclassified-as-capability). Test artifact vs real proof (T-tests-pollute-the-canonical-memory-store). Missing skill vs clean audit (T-orphan-skills-invisible-to-every-audit). Empty .text vs no reasoning (T-io-corpus-text-drops-reasoning). Crash vs honest refusal (T-intent-extractor-crash-masquerades-as-refusal). THIS IS THE MOST COMMON DEFECT IN THE QUEUE, not a one-off — and it is exactly the rule ratified the same day: THE CAUSE→SIGNAL MAP MUST BE INJECTIVE OVER EVERY PAIR OF CAUSES YOU WILL EVER NEED TO DISTINGUISH.

body.THE_FIX: T-sprint-tickets-with-no-intention-cannot-be-proven — gate the triage→sprint transition. It must reject MISSING, EMPTY, and DEGENERATE intentions (`TBD`/`unknown`/whitespace) — a bare presence-check passes all three, and `unknown` is precisely what a broken extractor writes. THE RED RUN IS NOW RECORDED AND IS NOT HYPOTHETICAL: 149 tickets, measured 2026-07-13, kicked back.
body.the_invariant_now_true_for_the_first_tim: sprint = 163 tickets, ALL 163 carrying an intention. The gate must now HOLD this, not achieve it.
body.falsifier: If a ticket ever reaches `sprint` again with a missing, empty, or degenerate intention, the gate is decorative and this FAIL was not learned from — only tidied up.

core_values: CP1 (a ticket with no intention closes posing as done)
CP4 (the nuisance stays visible or it sucks silently forever)
